title: Makefile: add install.nobuild target to install ONLY.
body:
Current behaviour is `make install` builds binary as well as installs it. This stays the same. But in scenario when one just want to install and not compile, there was no option but to patch source.

Adding new target accomodates such scenario.
Idea from @lsm5 https://github.com/podman-container-tools/skopeo/pull/2172#issuecomment-1838559541

comments.body:
> But in scenario when one just want to install and not compile

@rahilarious Are there cases where this is needed? For example, the rpm's install stage doesn't rebuild the binary anymore. See https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/pull/6785#pullrequestreview-4121014009 . Would that not apply here too?

> > But in scenario when one just want to install and not compile
> 
> @rahilarious Are there cases where this is needed? For example, the rpm's install stage doesn't rebuild the binary anymore. See [#6785 (review)](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/pull/6785#pullrequestreview-4121014009) . Would that not apply here too?

yes. It doesn't recompile but in Gentoo running executing build commands (i.e. `go build`) itself is prohibited in installation phase. So I had to patch source. But with this PR merged, I can just `make install.nobuild` & make patch redundant. 

I would've preferrred to just remove `bin/buildah` from `install:` like we do in Podman but, to not _break_ anything in minor update, introduced new target
